findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: '2 cfs' (13x); '5 cfs' (8x); '31 cfs' (7x).

findings.description: Duplicate nested <main> elements in HTML structure
findings.evidence: The HTML contains <main> nested inside another <main>, which is invalid HTML structure: <body><main><main>...</main></main></body>

findings.description: The second 'Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study' section heading is repeated identically from the first, whereas in the PDF these are continuation slides with the same title but different bullet content. While the content under each is different and correct, the identical h2 headings without differentiation could cause confusion.
findings.evidence: Both page 2 and page 3 of the PDF use the same title 'Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study'. The HTML reproduces both as identical <h2> headings, which is faithful to the source but noted for clarity.

findings.description: Some transect section headings are inconsistently structured - some include the full 'Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study' prefix and others do not
findings.evidence:
Transect #2 heading is just 'Transect #2, Run' while Transect #3 heading is 'Eno River State Park Ecological Flow Study
Transect #3, Riffle'. In the PDF, Transect #2 (page 8) does not have the full title header in the same way as others, so this follows the source.

findings.description: Transect #7 cross-section profile chart is missing from the HTML. Pages 18-19 show Transect #7 photos and an upstream view, but no cross-section graph is provided for Transect #7 (unlike all other transects which have accompanying cross-section graphs).
findings.evidence: The PDF does not appear to include a cross-section graph for Transect #7 either - the PDF goes from the Transect #7 photos (page 18) to the upstream view (page 19) to Transect #8 (page 20). So this is not a conversion error but a characteristic of the source.
